message:
🐛 Fix intermittent rocket scroll: PTY-level backpressure instead of dropping data (#184)

Layer 4 backpressure was silently dropping PTY data when the client
signaled pause — node-pty kept reading and emitting events that were
discarded. On resume, the terminal received data mid-stream with gaps,
causing garbled redraws and a feedback loop.

Now calls pty.pause()/resume() to apply real OS-level backpressure:
kernel PTY buffer fills, writing process blocks, zero data loss.
Any in-flight data (already-queued events) is buffered and flushed
on resume. PTY is always resumed on WebSocket close to prevent
stale pauses on reconnect.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
